source_jobs.title: Business Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARADIGM

Paradigm is a new type of spreadsheet for automating manual research with agents. We are based in San Francisco and is backed by 7M+ from General Catalyst, Y Combinator, and the best builder/operators out there including cofounders of Ramp, Dropbox, and more.

Our team is small and talent-dense. We are serial builders who have worked and studied at Google, MIT, Microsoft, Penn, Citadel, Bridgewater, Vercel, and more.

The spreadsheet interface is just the beginning. Our mission is to build a new type of agent-centric workspace that massively increases the productivity of knowledge workers. If you want to work on frontier AI systems that tackle meaningful real-world challenges and push the boundaries of what intelligent software can do, apply to join us.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll operate as a high-trust generalist embedded across the company wherever the highest-leverage problems exist. Projects will vary constantly: helping launch new product lines, supporting enterprise deployments, driving operational strategy, building internal systems and analytics infrastructure, supporting strategic partnerships and GTM initiatives, improving recruiting operations, or helping the company navigate critical scaling moments.

This is not a narrow operations role. You’ll work directly with leadership across product, engineering, go-to-market, and operations to help Paradigm scale quickly and effectively. Scope and ownership will be significant from day one. You’ll be expected to move fast, operate independently, and drive outcomes in ambiguous environments with very little structure.

This role is designed for people who want to be close to the hardest and most important problems inside a fast-growing AI company. You’ll build context across every part of the business and become a trusted operator that teams rely on. For people who excel here, the path naturally evolves into functional leadership or owning major parts of the company over time.



In this role, you will:

 - Own high-priority cross-functional projects end-to-end, from scoping and execution through rollout and iteration

 - Work directly with leadership to identify bottlenecks, operational gaps, and opportunities across the business

 - Build systems, processes, and internal tooling that help Paradigm scale efficiently

 - Support strategic initiatives across product, operations, enterprise deployments, growth, partnerships, recruiting, and finance as needed

 - Pull, analyze, and operationalize data to drive decision-making and improve execution

 - Build strong relationships across the organization and become a trusted partner to both technical and non-technical teams

 - Operate effectively in highly ambiguous environments where priorities shift quickly and context changes often

 - Step into whatever work is most important to the company, even when it falls outside a formal job description
   
   

Requirements for the role:

 - 3–5 years of experience in consulting, investment banking, business operations, strategy, product operations, or a high-growth technology company

 - Demonstrated ability to operate autonomously and execute effectively with minimal oversight

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

 - Excellent communication skills across both technical and non-technical audiences

 - High comfort with ambiguity, rapid iteration, and fast-moving environments

 - Track record of exceptional performance and high ownership in previous roles

 - Strong operational instincts and willingness to dive into details when necessary
   
   

You might excel if you…

 - Have worked as an early employee at a startup and operated far beyond your formal role

 - Gave experience as a Chief of Staff, BizOps lead, founder associate, or strategic operator at a scaling company

 - Gave a technical background in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or physics, even if your career path became more business-oriented

 - Enjoy solving ambiguous operationa

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARADIGM

Our mission is to build a new type of workspace that lets every knowledge worker leverage the power of LLMs to scale immense amounts of research in a simple and intuitive UI. Paradigm lets users utilize its purpose-built infrastructure to orchestrate hundreds of thousands of agents to process millions of tokens per minute, compressing days of research into seconds.

Our team must confront and solve problems unique to parallelizing agents at this magnitude, not limited to: race conditions for 10^5 agents writing to a data model simultaneously, realtime transmission of agent outputs across all services to the client, optimizing for both write throughput and in-memory footprint for each agent.

What we’re looking for

 - 7+ years of experience as a software engineer.

 - Consistent record as primary individual contributor to several medium to large projects.

 - Managed, designed and deployed distributed services in a production environment.

 - Seen as a resource by peers who can help debug and answer questions.

What can set you apart

 - We want your skills, not your title. We will prioritize those with a strong, demonstrated skillset over those who have learned to effectively climb the corporate ladder.

 - You know how to convey complex concepts clearly, can facilitate difficult conversations, and ensure everyone is on the same page.

 - Prior experience with: Go, Redis, PubSub, RPC, Postgres, S3, Temporal.

What we believe

Our best work is done in person. The team works together 5 days a week in our office in SF.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARADIGM

Paradigm is a new type of spreadsheet for automating manual research with agents. We are based in San Francisco and is backed by 7M+ from General Catalyst, Y Combinator, and the best builder/operators out there including cofounders of Ramp, Dropbox, and more.

Our team is small and talent-dense. We are serial builders who have worked and studied at Google, MIT, Microsoft, Penn, Citadel, Bridgewater, Vercel, and more.

The spreadsheet interface is just the beginning. Our mission is to build a new type of agent-centric workspace that massively increases the productivity of knowledge workers. If you want to work on frontier AI systems that tackle meaningful real-world challenges and push the boundaries of what intelligent software can do, apply to join us.




ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Develop and own Paradigm’s core messaging, positioning, and narrative across enterprise, financial services, and AI-native workflow use cases

 - Translate complex product capabilities into clear, compelling, and technically credible messaging tailored to different customer personas and industries

 - Produce high-quality customer-facing content across multiple mediums including customer stories, launch content, one-pagers, solution briefs, decks, battlecards, landing pages, and product demos

 - Partner closely with Product, Design, Engineering, and Sales to shape how new features, workflows, and launches are introduced to the market

 - Build sales enablement materials that directly support pipeline generation and enterprise deal cycles

 - Develop industry- and persona-specific narratives grounded in real customer workflows, outcomes, and competitive differentiation

 - Work directly with customers to turn successful implementations into reusable proof points, case studies, and co-marketing campaigns

 - Support major product launches and campaigns across web, email, events, outbound, and growth channels

 - Collaborate closely with account executives and solutions teams to understand customer objections, competitive dynamics, and what messaging actually moves deals forward

 - Help define Paradigm’s competitive positioning within AI tooling, enterprise software, and workflow automation markets

 - Create and maintain scalable messaging frameworks, product documentation, and launch processes as the company grows
   
   

Requirements for the role:

 - 2+ years of experience in product marketing, B2B marketing, growth, product, or another high-ownership operating role at a fast-paced company

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with a strong instinct for storytelling and positioning

 - Experience creating customer-facing collateral that sales teams actively use and customers resonate with

 - Ability to distill highly technical products and workflows into simple, benefit-driven messaging for different audiences

 - Strong product intuition and curiosity about AI, automation, enterprise software, and technical workflows

 - Experience working cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Design, and Revenue teams

 - Comfort speaking with technical operators, executives, and enterprise stakeholders

 - Ability to operate with high autonomy, move quickly, and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously

 - Strong attention to detail and high standards for quality across both strategy and execution

 - Basic design and visual communication sensibility

 - Willingness to travel occasionally for customer visits, launches, conferences, and content production
   
   

You might excel if you…

 - Have built marketing or field programs at a high-growth startup

 - Enjoy designing executive experiences and creating moments that differentiate a brand

 - Love working directly with customers and revenue teams to influence pipeline and close deals

 - Thrive when traveling, hosting, problem-solving, and adapting in real time

 - Are highly organized and operationally minded while still deeply creative

 - Are naturally curious abou

source_jobs.title: Growth Generalist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARADIGM

Paradigm is a new type of spreadsheet for automating manual research with agents. We are based in San Francisco and is backed by 7M+ from General Catalyst, Y Combinator, and the best builder/operators out there including cofounders of Ramp, Dropbox, and more.

Our team is small and talent-dense. We are serial builders who have worked and studied at Google, MIT, Microsoft, Penn, Citadel, Bridgewater, Vercel, and more.

The spreadsheet interface is just the beginning. Our mission is to build a new type of agent-centric workspace that massively increases the productivity of knowledge workers. If you want to work on frontier AI systems that tackle meaningful real-world challenges and push the boundaries of what intelligent software can do, apply to join us.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a growth generalist, you will be responsible for engaging with prospects and sourcing new opportunities. You will be a crucial contributor to Paradigm's revenue growth and development.

You’ll work closely with the go-to-market team to accelerate the sales cycle and help build one of the fastest-growing AI startups.

Ideal candidates will have an understanding of our technology, sales/applicable experience, and exhibit the drive and grit required to win consistently in a fast-paced, high-intensity environment.



In this role, you will:

 - Research, prospect, and engage high-level prospects.

 - Learn to navigate complex organizational structures, and identify executive sponsors & champions.

 - Discover and understand the business objectives of your customers, and ability to engage with key stakeholders via cold outreach.

 - Iterate and think through growth strategy as we optimize each part of the GTM motion.

 - Provide timely and insightful input back to the product team.
   
   

Requirements for the role:

 - We’re looking for A-players with insanely high adaptability, who have or feel they would thrive in an intense early-stage company environment.

 - 1+ years in a growth role with a track record of success & high performance OR proven track record of success in another domain.

 - Willingness to travel onsite to prospects/customers, around the US and internationally.

 - Ability to learn and become an expert quickly, on both our product and how prospects/customers use AI research tools to have real, quantifiable impacts on their business.

 - Team player – We win together.

 - Self-starter – Hunger to dream up, plan, and execute tasks independently.

 - Bonus: familiarity AI-native products and usage-based pricing.
   
   

You might excel if you…

 - Are highly ambitious or have a competitive background (athletics, debate, etc.).

 - Have found success in another high-growth, fast-paced environment.

 - Have experience with software engineering, studied computer science, or another technical background.
   
   

What we believe…

Our best work is done in person. The team works together 5 days a week in our office in SF.

notes: Small team with a strong engineering focus, backed by prominent investors.
